_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 16:21:27Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 16:21:32Z)**_

The change adds a single presentational React component (`NoVesselsAssignedEmptyState`) inside a Vercel mockup file. The only attacker-influenceable input is the `userName` prop, which is rendered as a JSX text node via a template literal:

```jsx
{userName ? `Hi ${userName}, you ` : 'You '}
```

React auto-escapes string children — there is no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no URL/href/src sink, no eval, no template engine, no logging/telemetry, no filesystem/shell/SQL sink. The component only sets `style` props from a static `COLORS` object and `className` strings (no user input flows into them). No new entry points, validators, registries, or trust boundaries are introduced.

No source→sink path with security impact exists in this diff.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 16:21:34Z)**_

No findings. The diff only adds a presentational React component that renders `userName` as an auto-escaped JSX text node; there are no injection, authorization, or data-flow sinks introduced.
